translations.literal: Afterward Protector-General Gan Yanshou, deputy colonel Chen Tang raised Wuji colonel Western Regions various states' troops reach Kangju, executed destroyed Zhizhi Chanyu; words are in "Traditions of Gan Yanshou and Chen Tang".
translations.idiomatic: Later Protector-General Gan Yanshou and Deputy Colonel Chen Tang mobilized the Wuji colonel and western allies against Kangju and destroyed Zhizhi; the story is told in "Traditions of Gan Yanshou and Chen Tang".

sentences.zh: 是歲，元帝建昭三年也。

translations.literal: This year—Emperor Yuandi Jianzhao third year.
translations.idiomatic: That was the third year of Jianzhao under Emperor Yuan.

sentences.zh: 至成帝時，康居遣子侍漢，貢獻，然自以絕遠，獨驕嫚，不肯與諸國相望。

translations.literal: Reach Emperor Cheng time; Kangju sent son attend Han, tribute offer; yet self-deemed utterly remote; alone arrogant contemptuous; unwilling with various states mutually regard.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Cheng Kangju sent a prince as hostage and offered tribute, yet deemed itself beyond reach and alone refused to treat other states as peers.

sentences.zh: 都護郭舜數上言：「本匈奴盛時，非以兼有烏孫、康居故也；

translations.literal: Protector-General Guo Shun repeatedly memorialized: Originally Xiongnu flourishing time, not because also possessing Wusun, Kangju reason;
translations.idiomatic: Protector-General Guo Shun argued repeatedly: At the height of Xiongnu power they did not owe their strength to holding both Wusun and Kangju;

sentences.zh: 及其稱臣妾，非以失二國也。

translations.literal: And when they called themselves subjects concubines, not because lost two states.
translations.idiomatic: and when they submitted as vassals it was not because they had forfeited those two realms.

sentences.zh: 漢雖皆受其質子，然三國內相輸遺，交通如故，亦相候司，見便則發；

translations.literal: Han although all received their hostages; yet three states internally mutually convey gifts; communicate as before; also mutually spy watch; see opportunity then strike;
translations.idiomatic: Han may hold hostages from all three, yet they still smuggle gifts, traffic as before, spy on one another, and strike whenever advantage appears;

sentences.zh: 合不能相親信，離不能相臣役。

translations.literal: United cannot mutually intimate trust; divided cannot mutually subject serve.
translations.idiomatic: united they never trust one another, divided they cannot subjugate each other.

sentences.zh: 以今言之，結配烏孫竟未有益，反為中國生事。

translations.literal: Using today speak—marriage alliance Wusun finally not yet benefit; instead for Central States produced trouble.
translations.idiomatic: Judged by present facts, the marriage league with Wusun has brought no gain and only stirs trouble for the interior.

sentences.zh: 然烏孫既結在前，今與匈奴俱稱臣，義不可距。

translations.literal: Yet Wusun since alliance earlier; now with Xiongnu together call themselves subjects; righteousness cannot refuse.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Wusun was tied to us first, and now both it and the Xiongnu style themselves vassals—decency forbids turning them away.

sentences.zh: 而康居驕黠，訖不肯拜使者。

translations.literal: But Kangju arrogant crafty; utterly unwilling bow to envoys.
translations.idiomatic: Kangju remains arrogant and sly and still refuses to bow to Han envoys.

sentences.zh: 都護吏至其國，坐之烏孫諸使下，王及貴人先飲食已，乃飲啖都護吏，故為無所省以誇旁國。

translations.literal: Protector-General clerks reach its state; seat them below Wusun various envoys; king and nobles first drink eat finished; then drink eat Protector-General clerks; deliberately act without regard to boast neighboring states.
translations.idiomatic: When Protector-General officers arrive they are seated beneath Wusun embassies while the king and nobles dine first and only then feed Han clerks—deliberate insult to impress neighbors.

sentences.zh: 以此度之，何故遣子入侍？

translations.literal: By this measure—why reason send son enter attendance?
translations.idiomatic: Measured thus, why send a prince as hostage?

sentences.zh: 其欲賈市為好，辭之詐也。

translations.literal: Their desire market trade as friendship—speech its deceit.
translations.idiomatic: They crave commerce masked as friendship—that plea is a lie.

sentences.zh: 匈奴百蠻大國，今事漢其備，聞康居不拜，且使單于有自下之意，宜歸其侍子，絕勿復使，以章漢家不通無禮之國。

translations.literal: Xiongnu hundred barbarians great state; now serve Han its full ceremony; hear Kangju not bow; moreover cause Chanyu have self-lowering intention; ought return their attendant son; cut off never again send; thereby manifest Han house does not communicate discourteous states.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu head the barbarian league and now observe full courtesy toward Han. If they hear Kangju refuses to bow, the Chanyu may think he too can ease his deference. Return Kangju's hostage, halt embassies, and show that Han breaks with states that scorn ritual.

sentences.zh: 敦煌、酒泉小郡及南道八國，給使者往來人、馬、驢、橐駝食，皆苦之。

translations.literal: Dunhuang, Jiuquan minor commanderies and southern route eight states; supply envoys coming-going men, horses, donkeys, camel food—all suffer it.
translations.idiomatic: Dunhuang, Jiuquan, and the eight southern-route states strain to provision every envoy's men, mounts, donkeys, and camels.

sentences.zh: 空罷耗所過，送迎驕黠絕遠之國，非至計也。

translations.literal: Empty exhaust waste what passes; escort welcome arrogant crafty utterly remote states—not supreme plan.
translations.idiomatic: Emptying the frontier to pamper arrogant realms beyond reach is no wise policy.

sentences.zh: 」漢為其新通，重致遠人。

translations.literal: Closed-quote Han because its newly opened communications; honor summon distant people.
translations.idiomatic: Even so, Han prized newcomers from afar and indulged them.

sentences.zh: 終羈縻而未絕。

translations.literal: Finally loose-rein yet not cut off.
translations.idiomatic: Ties stayed slack but never quite snapped.

sentences.zh: 其康居西北可二千里，有奄蔡國。

translations.literal: Its Kangju northwest about two thousand li; has Yancai state.
translations.idiomatic: Some two thousand li northwest of Kangju lies Yancai.

sentences.zh: 控弦者十餘萬人。

translations.literal: Bow-drawers over hundred thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: More than a hundred thousand warriors draw the bow there.

sentences.zh: 與康居同俗。

translations.literal: With Kangju same customs.
translations.idiomatic: Their customs mirror Kangju's.

sentences.zh: 臨大澤，無崖，蓋北海雲。

translations.literal: Borders great marsh; without shore; probably northern sea—it is said.
translations.idiomatic: It fronts a shoreless inland sea said to be the Northern Ocean.

sentences.zh: 康居有小王五：一曰蘇薤王，治蘇薤城，去都護五千七百七十六里，去陽關八千二十五里；

translations.literal: Kangju has petty kings five: first called Suxie king, rules Suxie city, from Protector-General five thousand seven hundred seventy-six li, from Yang Pass eight thousand twenty-five li;
translations.idiomatic: Kangju maintains five junior kings: first, the lord of Suxie at Suxie—5,776 li from the Protector-General and 8,025 li from Yang Pass;

sentences.zh: 二曰附墨王，治附墨城，去都護五千七百六十七里，去陽關八千二十五里；

translations.literal: Second called Fumo king, rules Fumo city, from Protector-General five thousand seven hundred sixty-seven li, from Yang Pass eight thousand twenty-five li;
translations.idiomatic: second, the lord of Fumo at Fumo—5,767 li from the Protector-General and 8,025 li from Yang Pass;

sentences.zh: 三曰窳匿王，治窳匿城，去都護五千二百六十六里，去陽關七千五百二十五里；

translations.literal: Third called Yuni king, rules Yuni city, from Protector-General five thousand two hundred sixty-six li, from Yang Pass seven thousand five hundred twenty-five li;
translations.idiomatic: third, the lord of Yuni at Yuni—5,266 li from the Protector-General and 7,525 li from Yang Pass;

sentences.zh: 四曰罽王，治罽城，去都護六千二百九十六里，去陽關八千五百五十五里；

translations.literal: Fourth called Ji king, rules Ji city, from Protector-General six thousand two hundred ninety-six li, from Yang Pass eight thousand five hundred fifty-five li;
translations.idiomatic: fourth, the lord of Ji at Ji—6,296 li from the Protector-General and 8,555 li from Yang Pass;

sentences.zh: 五曰奧鞬王，治奧鞬城，去都護六千九百六里，去陽關八千三百五十五里。

translations.literal: Fifth called Aojian king, rules Aojian city, from Protector-General six thousand nine hundred six li, from Yang Pass eight thousand three hundred fifty-five li.
translations.idiomatic: fifth, the lord of Aojian at Aojian—6,906 li from the Protector-General and 8,355 li from Yang Pass.

sentences.zh: 凡五王，屬康居。

translations.literal: In total five kings—belong to Kangju.
translations.idiomatic: These five lords all answered to Kangju.

sentences.zh: 大宛國，王治貴山城，去長安萬二千五百五十里。

translations.literal: Great Yuan state—king rules Guishan city—twelve thousand five hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom of Ferghana ruled from Guishan, twelve thousand five hundred fifty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶六萬，口三十萬，勝兵六萬人。

translations.literal: Households sixty thousand, mouths three hundred thousand, victors-in-arms sixty thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: It counted 60,000 households, 300,000 people, and 60,000 soldiers.

sentences.zh: 副王、輔國王各一人。

translations.literal: Deputy king, state-supporting king—each one person.
translations.idiomatic: There were one deputy king and one king who aided the realm.

sentences.zh: 東至都護治所四千三十一里，北至康居卑闐城千五百一十里，西南至大月氏六百九十里。

translations.literal: East to Protector-General governing seat four thousand thirty-one li; north to Kangju Beitian city one thousand five hundred ten li; southwest to Great Yuezhi six hundred ninety li.
translations.idiomatic: It lay 4,031 li west of the Protector-General's headquarters, 1,510 li south of Kangju's Beitian, and 690 li northeast of the Great Yuezhi.

sentences.zh: 北與康居、南與大月氏接，土地風氣物類民俗與大月氏、安息同。

translations.literal: North adjoins Kangju, south adjoins Great Yuezhi; land soil wind qi things kinds folk customs—with Great Yuezhi, Anxi same.
translations.idiomatic: Kangju bordered it on the north and the Great Yuezhi on the south; terrain, climate, products, and customs matched those of the Yuezhi and Parthia.

sentences.zh: 大宛左右以蒲陶為酒，富人藏酒至萬餘石，久者至數十歲不敗。

translations.literal: Great Yuan vicinity uses grapes for wine; rich men store wine reaching over ten thousand shi; aged reaches several tens years not spoiling.
translations.idiomatic: The Ferghana valley fermented wine from grapes; wealthy households cellared more than ten thousand shi, and the oldest vintages kept for decades without turning.

sentences.zh: 俗耆酒，馬耆目宿。

translations.literal: Custom esteems wine; horses esteem alfalfa.
translations.idiomatic: The people love their wine and their horses love alfalfa pasture.

sentences.zh: 宛別邑七十餘城，多善馬。

translations.literal: Yuan separate towns seventy-odd cities; many fine horses.
translations.idiomatic: More than seventy satellite towns bred excellent horses.

sentences.zh: 馬汗血，言其先天馬子也。

translations.literal: Horses sweat blood—said their former heavenly horse offspring.
translations.idiomatic: The famed blood-sweating horses were said to descend from celestial stock.

sentences.zh: 張騫始為武帝言之，上遣使者持千金及金馬，以請宛善馬。

translations.literal: Zhang Qian first for Emperor Wu spoke it; superior dispatched envoys bearing thousand gold and gold horse, in order request Yuan fine horses.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Qian first told Emperor Wu of them; the throne then sent envoys with a fortune in gold and a golden horse to buy Ferghana's best mounts.

sentences.zh: 宛王以漢絕遠，大兵不能至，愛其寶馬不肯與。

translations.literal: Yuan king because Han utterly remote, great army cannot arrive; loves its treasure horses unwilling give.
translations.idiomatic: The king reckoned Han too distant for a major expedition and refused to part with his prized horses.

sentences.zh: 漢使妄言，宛遂攻殺漢使，取其財物。

translations.literal: Han envoy reckless speech; Yuan thereupon attacked killed Han envoy, took their goods wealth.
translations.idiomatic: Provoked by sharp words from the Han mission, Ferghana attacked, slew the envoys, and seized their goods.

sentences.zh: 於是天子遣貳師將軍李廣利將兵前後十餘萬人伐宛，連四年。

translations.literal: Thereupon Son of Heaven dispatched Ershi General Li Guangli lead troops before-after over hundred thousand persons attack Yuan, continuous four years.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor therefore ordered General Ershi Li Guangli west with well over a hundred thousand men in a campaign that dragged on four years.

sentences.zh: 宛人斬其王毋寡首，獻馬三千匹，漢軍乃還，語在《張騫傳》。

translations.literal: Yuan men beheaded their king Mugua's head; offered horses three thousand pairs; Han army then returned; words are in "Traditions of Zhang Qian".
translations.idiomatic: The Ferghana elite slew King Mugua and sent three thousand horses; only then did the Han army withdraw; the account is in "Traditions of Zhang Qian".

sentences.zh: 貳師既斬宛王，更立貴人素遇漢善者名昧蔡為宛王。

translations.literal: Ershi having beheaded Yuan king; further established noble always treated Han well named Meicai as Yuan king.
translations.idiomatic: After Ershi executed the king he enthroned the noble Meicai, who had long dealt kindly with Han.

sentences.zh: 後歲餘，宛貴人以為「昧蔡諂，使我國遇屠」，相與共殺昧蔡，立毋寡弟蟬封為王，遣子入侍，質於漢，漢因使使賂賜鎮撫之。

translations.literal: After year surplus; Yuan nobles thought "Meicai flatters, caused our state meet massacre"; mutually together killed Meicai; established Mugua younger brother Chanfeng as king; sent son enter attendance; hostage at Han; Han thereupon sent envoys bribe gifts pacify comfort them.
translations.idiomatic: More than a year later the Ferghana elite charged that Meicai had truckled to Han and "brought slaughter on our land"; they assassinated him and raised Mugua's brother Chanfeng, dispatching a prince as hostage while Han sent gifts to steady the new regime.

sentences.zh: 又發使十餘輩，抵宛西諸國求奇物，因風諭以伐宛之威。

translations.literal: Further dispatched envoys over ten parties; reach Yuan west various states seek strange goods; thereby wind instruct with attack Yuan's might.
translations.idiomatic: Han also sent a dozen missions west of Ferghana for curiosities and to advertise the power that had crushed the valley.

sentences.zh: 宛王蟬封與漢約，歲獻天馬二匹。

translations.literal: Yuan king Chanfeng with Han covenanted; yearly offer heavenly horses two pairs.
translations.idiomatic: King Chanfeng pledged two heavenly horses in annual tribute.

sentences.zh: 漢使采蒲陶、目宿種歸。

translations.literal: Han envoys gathered grape, alfalfa seeds returned.
translations.idiomatic: Han agents collected vine cuttings and alfalfa seed for the trip home.

sentences.zh: 天子以天馬多，又外國使來眾，益種蒲陶、目宿離宮館旁，極望焉。

translations.literal: Son of Heaven because heavenly horses many; also foreign envoys come multitude; increased plant grapes alfalfa detached palace lodges beside; extreme vista there.
translations.idiomatic: With heavenly horses abundant and foreign embassies thronging the capital, the court planted grapes and alfalfa around the suburban lodges until they stretched to the horizon.

sentences.zh: 自宛以西至安息國，雖頗異言，然大同，自相曉知也。

translations.literal: From Yuan west to Anxi state; although somewhat different speech; yet greatly same; mutually self understand know.
translations.idiomatic: From Ferghana west to Parthia the tongues differ somewhat yet remain mutually intelligible.

sentences.zh: 其人皆深目，多鬚髯。

translations.literal: Its people all deep eyes; many beards whiskers.
translations.idiomatic: The inhabitants are deep-eyed and heavily bearded.

sentences.zh: 善賈市，爭分銖。

translations.literal: Skilled at marketing; contend over fen zhu.
translations.idiomatic: They are sharp traders who haggle over the smallest copper.

sentences.zh: 貴女子，女子所言，丈夫乃決正。

translations.literal: Esteem women; women what speak—husbands then decide correct.
translations.idiomatic: Women are honored: only after a wife speaks may husbands settle a dispute.

sentences.zh: 其地無絲漆，不知鑄鐵器。

translations.literal: Its land lacks silk lacquer; does not know cast iron tools.
translations.idiomatic: The region produced neither silk nor lacquer and once knew nothing of iron casting.

sentences.zh: 及漢使亡卒降，教鑄作它兵器。

translations.literal: Reach Han envoy deserting soldiers surrender; taught cast make other weapons arms.
translations.idiomatic: Deserters from Han missions taught them to forge other kinds of arms.

sentences.zh: 得漢黃白金，輒以為器，不用為幣。

translations.literal: Obtain Han yellow white metal; always make vessels; not use as currency.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever they acquired Han gold or silver they worked it into vessels rather than coin.

sentences.zh: 自烏孫以西至安息，近匈奴。

translations.literal: From Wusun west to Anxi—near Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: West of Wusun through Parthia the steppe presses close to the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 匈奴嘗困月氏，故匈奴使持單于一信到國，國傳送食，不敢留苦。

translations.literal: Xiongnu once distressed Yuezhi; therefore Xiongnu envoy bearing Chanyu one credential arrives state; state relays conveys food; dare not detain harshness.
translations.idiomatic: Because the Xiongnu had broken the Yuezhi, any envoy bearing the Chanyu's token was relay-fed through every state and never hindered.

sentences.zh: 及至漢使，非出幣物不得食，不市畜不得騎，所以然者，以遠漢，而漢多財物，故必市乃得所欲。

translations.literal: When reach Han envoys—without presenting goods wealth cannot eat; without buying livestock cannot ride; reason thus—because distant from Han; yet Han abundant goods wealth; therefore must buy then obtain what desired.
translations.idiomatic: Han envoys had to pay for food and buy mounts; distant though Han was, its wealth was legendary, so locals insisted on trade before granting anything.

sentences.zh: 及呼韓邪單于朝漢，後鹹尊漢矣。

translations.literal: When Huhanye Chanyu courted Han; afterward all honored Han.
translations.idiomatic: Once Huhanye Chanyu attended the Han court, every western state learned to honor Han.

sentences.zh: 桃槐國，王去長安萬一千八十里。

translations.literal: Taohuai state—king from Chang'an eleven thousand eighty li.
translations.idiomatic: Taohuai's royal seat stood eleven thousand eighty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶七百，口五千，勝兵千人。

translations.literal: Households seven hundred, mouths five thousand, victors-in-arms one thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: It recorded 700 households, 5,000 people, and 1,000 troops.

sentences.zh: 休循國，王治鳥飛谷，在蔥嶺西，去長安萬二百一十里。

translations.literal: Xiuxun state—king rules Bird-Flight valley; lies Congling west—ten thousand two hundred ten li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Xiuxun ruled Bird-Flight Valley west of the Pamirs, ten thousand two hundred ten li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶三百五十八，口千三十，勝兵四百八十人。

translations.literal: Households three hundred fifty-eight, mouths one thousand thirty, victors-in-arms four hundred eighty persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had 358 households, 1,030 inhabitants, and 480 soldiers.

sentences.zh: 東至都護治所三千一百二十一里，至捐毒衍敦谷二百六十裡，西北至大宛國九百二十里，西至大月氏千六百一十里。

translations.literal: East to Protector-General governing seat three thousand one hundred twenty-one li; reach Juandu Yandun valley two hundred sixty li; northwest to Great Yuan state nine hundred twenty li; west to Great Yuezhi one thousand six hundred ten li.
translations.idiomatic: It lay 3,121 li west of the Protector-General, 260 li from Juandu's Yandun Valley, 920 li southeast of Ferghana, and 1,610 li east of the Great Yuezhi.

sentences.zh: 民俗衣服類烏孫，因畜隨水草，本故塞種也。

translations.literal: Folk customs clothing resembles Wusun; rely livestock follow water grasses; origin former Sai stock.
translations.idiomatic: Dress and custom resemble Wusun's pastoral ways along seasonal pastures—they stem from the Sai peoples.

sentences.zh: 捐毒國，王治衍敦谷，去長安九千八百六十里。

translations.literal: Juandu state—king rules Yandun valley—nine thousand eight hundred sixty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Juandu ruled Yandun Valley, nine thousand eight hundred sixty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶三百八十，口千一百，勝兵五百人。

translations.literal: Households three hundred eighty, mouths one thousand one hundred, victors-in-arms five hundred persons.
translations.idiomatic: It counted 380 households, 1,100 people, and 500 warriors.

sentences.zh: 東至都護治所二千八百六十一里。

translations.literal: East to Protector-General governing seat two thousand eight hundred sixty-one li.
translations.idiomatic: The Protector-General's headquarters lay 2,861 li to the east.

sentences.zh: 至疏勒。

translations.literal: Reaches Shule.
translations.idiomatic: That route reaches Shule.

sentences.zh: 南與蔥嶺屬，無人民。

translations.literal: South with Congling connects; lacks people folk.
translations.idiomatic: To the south it joins the Pamir wastes where no one dwells.

sentences.zh: 西上蔥領，則休循也。

translations.literal: West ascend Cong ridge—then Xiuxun.
translations.idiomatic: Westward up the Pamir ridge lies Xiuxun.

sentences.zh: 西北至大宛千三十里，北與烏孫接。

translations.literal: Northwest to Great Yuan one thousand thirty li; north adjoins Wusun.
translations.idiomatic: Ferghana is 1,030 li to the northwest and Wusun borders the north.

sentences.zh: 衣服類烏孫，隨水草，依蔥領，本塞種也。

translations.literal: Clothing resembles Wusun; follow water grasses; rely Cong ridge; origin Sai stock.
translations.idiomatic: Dress follows Wusun; herders roam the Pamir pastures—they too are Sai stock.

sentences.zh: 莎車國，王治莎車城，去長安九千九百五十里。

translations.literal: Shache state—king rules Shache city—nine thousand nine hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom of Shache ruled from its namesake capital, nine thousand nine hundred fifty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶二千三百三十九，口萬六千三百七十三，勝兵三千四十九人。

translations.literal: Households two thousand three hundred thirty-nine, mouths sixteen thousand three hundred seventy-three, victors-in-arms three thousand forty-nine persons.
translations.idiomatic: It comprised 2,339 households, 16,373 people, and 3,049 troops.

sentences.zh: 輔國侯、左右將、左右騎君、備西夜君各一人，都尉二人，譯長四人。

translations.literal: State-supporting marquis, left right generals, left right cavalry chiefs, equip Xiye chief—each one person; commandants two persons; chief interpreters four persons.
translations.idiomatic: Officials included one state-supporting marquis, one general and one cavalry chief on each flank, one minister for Xiye, two commandants, and four chief interpreters.

sentences.zh: 東北至都護治所四千七百四十六里，西至疏勒五百六十里，西南至蒲犁七百四十里。

translations.literal: Northeast to Protector-General governing seat four thousand seven hundred forty-six li; west to Shule five hundred sixty li; southwest to Puli seven hundred forty li.
translations.idiomatic: It stood 4,746 li northeast of the Protector-General's seat, 560 li east of Shule, and 740 li northeast of Puli.

sentences.zh: 有鐵山，出青玉。

translations.literal: Has Iron Mountain; produces green jade.
translations.idiomatic: Iron Mountain yields green jade.

sentences.zh: 疏勒國，王治疏勒城，去長安九千三百五十里。

translations.literal: Shule state—king rules Shule city—nine thousand three hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Shule ruled from its capital, nine thousand three hundred fifty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶千五百一十，口萬八千六百四十七，勝兵二千人。

translations.literal: Households one thousand five hundred ten, mouths eighteen thousand six hundred forty-seven, victors-in-arms two thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: It recorded 1,510 households, 18,647 inhabitants, and 2,000 soldiers.

sentences.zh: 疏勒侯、擊胡侯、輔國侯、都尉、左右將、左右騎君、左右譯長各一人。

translations.literal: Shule marquis, Strike Hu marquis, state-supporting marquis, commandant, left right generals, left right cavalry chiefs, left right chief interpreters—each one person.
translations.idiomatic: Its roster listed marquises of Shule, of striking the Hu, and of aiding the state, one commandant, paired generals and cavalry chiefs, and paired chief interpreters.

sentences.zh: 東至都護治所二千二百一十里，南至莎車五百六十里。

translations.literal: East to Protector-General governing seat two thousand two hundred ten li; south to Shache five hundred sixty li.
translations.idiomatic: The Protector-General lay 2,210 li to the east and Shache 560 li to the south.

sentences.zh: 有市列，西當大月氏、大宛、康居道也。

translations.literal: Has market rows; west faces Great Yuezhi, Great Yuan, Kangju road.
translations.idiomatic: Busy markets line the highway west toward the Yuezhi, Ferghana, and Kangju.

sentences.zh: 尉頭國，王治尉頭谷，去長安八千六百五十里。

translations.literal: Weitou state—king rules Weitou valley—eight thousand six hundred fifty li from Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Weitou ruled Weitou Valley, eight thousand six hundred fifty li from Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 戶三百，口二千三百，勝兵八百人。

translations.literal: Households three hundred, mouths two thousand three hundred, victors-in-arms eight hundred persons.
translations.idiomatic: It had 300 households, 2,300 people, and 800 warriors.

sentences.zh: 左右都尉各一人，左右騎君各一人。

translations.literal: Left right commandants each one person; left right cavalry chiefs each one person.
translations.idiomatic: There were one commandant and one cavalry chief on each flank.

sentences.zh: 東至都護治所千四百一十一里，南與疏勒接，山道不通，西至捐毒千三百一十四里，逕道馬行二日。

translations.literal: East to Protector-General governing seat one thousand four hundred eleven li; south adjoins Shule; mountain road does not connect; west to Juandu one thousand three hundred fourteen li; direct road horse journey two days.
translations.idiomatic: It lay 1,411 li west of the Protector-General; mountains block the road south to Shule, while a direct trail reaches Juandu in two days' ride.

sentences.zh: 田畜隨水草，衣服類烏孫。

translations.literal: Field livestock follow water grasses; clothing resembles Wusun.
translations.idiomatic: Farmers and herders follow the pastures; dress matches Wusun.
